methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: True Anomaly
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Hidden Level is a Series C defense & security company founded by Jeff Cole and Gary Dominicos, currently valued at $500M post-money with $130M raised to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables, consisting of 2 public companies and 8 private firms including True Anomaly, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $500M last post-money valuation, implying $850M. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company comparable set yielded a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $9.2B; peers at equivalent or adjacent stages include True Anomaly at $1.5B (Series C), Vannevar Labs and Dream Security each at $1.5B (Series B), alongside larger Series D assets like Chaos Industries at $4.5B and Saronic at $9.2B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $722M to $1.7B, with a median implied value of $1.1B. This range reflects meaningful tension between internal stage-progression mechanics and peer-relative positioning. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—driven partly by stage heterogeneity and company-specific de-risking—and the appropriate weighting of early-stage versus later-stage comparables in establishing fair value for a Series C asset.
